Computer-Use Skill (Session 10) + cua-driver
Context
The repo has a mature Browser skill (code/browser/skill.py) with a visible cascade (Layer 1 extract â†’ 2a deterministic â†’ 2b a11y â†’ 3 vision). There is no desktop/computer-use code today.
New dependency: cua-driver â€” a local desktop driver (Windows UI Automation, background/no-focus-steal) exposed as MCP stdio and CLI. It provides the assignment's start_recording, page (with electron_debugging_port), hotkey, get_window_state (capture modes ax / som / vision), and per-turn trajectory folders (turn-NNNNN/action.json, screenshot.png, app_state.json).
User choice: Wire cua-driver project-wide; each skill opts in via tools_allowed in agent_config.yaml. The computer skill is the primary cascade consumer; other skills (e.g. researcher) may use read-only cua tools when listed.
Primary OS: Windows. cua-driver is the official backend on Windows (not hand-rolled pyautogui / uiautomation).
ï؟¼
cua-driver infrastructure (all skills, opt-in)
Install prerequisite (not pip)
cua-driver is a signed binary, installed via PowerShell (installation docs):
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/trycua/cua/main/libs/cua-driver/scripts/install.ps1 | iex
cua-driver autostart          # Windows daemon in interactive session
cua-driver check_permissions  # UI Automation grants
Ship code/scripts/setup_cua_driver.ps1 wrapping the above for the course repo.
Daemon required for element-index workflows and start_recording (state lives in-process).
Shared Python wrapper â€” code/cua/
code/cua/
  __init__.py
  client.py       # CuaDriverClient â€” MCP stdio OR `cua-driver call` fallback
  tools.py        # Tool name constants + JSON schemas for mcp_runner merge
  recording.py    # start_recording / stop_recording wrappers
CuaDriverClient methods map 1:1 to MCP tools: launch_app, get_window_state, click, type_text, hotkey, page, start_recording, etc.
Dual MCP in code/mcp_runner.py
Today: single stdio server â†’ code/mcp_server.py.
Change: When a skill's tools_allowed includes any tool prefixed cua_ (or listed in a CUA_TOOL_NAMES set), open a second stdio session:
StdioServerParameters(command="cua-driver", args=["mcp"])
Dispatch routing:
â€¢ web_search, fetch_url, â€¦ â†’ eagv3 MCP (unchanged)
â€¢ cua_launch_app, cua_get_window_state, â€¦ â†’ cua-driver MCP (prefixed names avoid collisions)
Alternative: pass through unprefixed cua tool names if the skill's allowlist is disjoint from eagv3 tools (current eagv3 has no click/hotkey).
Per-skill opt-in â€” code/agent_config.yaml
Skill
tools_allowed (cua subset)

Sandbox Python
computer
(bypass â€” uses CuaDriverClient directly in cascade)
Full desktop stack
Computer skill does not go through mcp_runner tool loop (same pattern as browser); it calls CuaDriverClient internally.
code/skills.py tool payload
Extend tool_payload() to merge eagv3 schemas + cua schemas when skill lists cua tools.
ï؟¼
Five-layer cascade (desktop) â€” backed by cua-driver
Cascade discipline stays visible in code (code/computer/skill.py); each layer is a thin module calling cua-driver with the right capture_mode / tool.

Maps to cua-driver capture modes (docs):
â€¢ Layer 2b AX â†’ capture_mode=ax
â€¢ Layer 3 â†’ capture_mode=vision (or som when AX empty but screenshot needed)
â€¢ Default daemon config can stay som; computer skill overrides per layer call
Electron path: use cua launch_app with electron_debugging_port + page tool â€” not a separate Playwright CDP client (cua owns CDP). Still Layer 2b, path=electron.
Escalation: same as browser â€” first useful layer wins; metadata.force_path: read | hotkey | electron | ax | vision.
Snapshot invariant (from cua quickstart): get_window_state before and after every action; element indices stale across turns.
ï؟¼
Trajectory recording â€” native start_recording
Use cua-driver's MCP tool (docs) â€” not a custom recorder:
{
  "name": "start_recording",
  "arguments": {
    "output_dir": "state/sessions/<sid>/computer/trajectory_<ts>",
    "record_video": false
  }
}
Every subsequent cua action writes turn-NNNNN/ with action.json, screenshot.png, app_state.json, click.png (click family).
ComputerSkill.run():
1. start_recording(output_dir=...)
2. Run cascade
3. stop_recording on success/failure
4. Set ComputerOutput.trajectory_dir to that directory (evidence to submit)
Optional: merge gateway session id into a small manifest.json beside cua turns for cross-reference with /v1/cost/by_agent.
ï؟¼

ï؟¼
Package layout
code/cua/                    # NEW â€” shared cua-driver client (all opt-in skills)
  client.py
  tools.py
  recording.py

code/computer/               # NEW â€” cascade skill
  skill.py
  layer1_read.py
  layer2a_hotkey.py
  layer2b_electron.py        # cua page + CDP
  layer2b_ax.py              # element_index loop + V10Client chat
  layer3_vision.py           # vision capture_mode + V10Client vision
  fixtures/canvas_only.html
  scripts/launch_cursor_debug.ps1

code/scripts/setup_cua_driver.ps1
Reuse:
â€¢ code/browser/client.py â€” V10Client for LLM judgment in layers 2b/3 (same as browser)
â€¢ Do not add pyautogui, uiautomation, mss â€” cua-driver replaces them
ï؟¼
Orchestrator integration
Schema â€” code/schemas.py
ComputerPath = Literal["read", "hotkey", "electron", "ax", "vision"]

class ComputerOutput(BaseModel):
    app: str
    goal: str
    path: ComputerPath
    turns: int = 0
    result: str | None = None
    actions: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
    trajectory_dir: str   # cua-driver start_recording output_dir
Catalogue â€” computer entry + optional researcher cua tools (see table above)
Dispatch â€” code/skills.py if skill.name == "computer": branch (mirror browser)
Prompts â€” code/prompts/planner.md, code/prompts/computer.md
ï؟¼
Dependencies â€” code/pyproject.toml
â€¢ No new pip packages for desktop automation (cua-driver is external binary)
â€¢ Existing: mcp, playwright (browser only), httpx
â€¢ Document cua-driver in code/COMPUTER_VALIDATION.md prerequisites section
ï؟¼

Risk notes
Risk
Mitigation
cua-driver not on PATH

UI Automation permissions
cua-driver check_permissions in setup script

cua-driver autostart; computer skill checks daemon before cascade
Cursor CDP
electron_debugging_port on launch_app; document manual launch fallback
Dual MCP complexity
Prefix cua tools; unit-test dispatch routing
Browser skill unchanged
Web stays on Playwright; desktop on cua-driver
ï؟¼
Out of scope
â€¢ No changes to code/flow.py graph logic
â€¢ No mandatory cua tools on planner/formatter (opt-in only)
â€¢ No record_video: true unless user requests (default false per assignment)
â€¢ Linux/macOS computer tasks in v1 (cua supports them; validate Windows first)

Explored
lints
Two separate issues in your screenshots â€” both addressed.
Canvas (path=vision, 503)
The canvas task is reaching the vision layer correctly now. The failure is the gateway returning 503 on /v1/vision (provider unavailable or rate-limited). Your .env has AGENT_LLM_PROVIDER=oai, which should pin OpenAI for vision calls.
Try: restart the gateway, then rerun the canvas preset:
cd llm_gatewayV10; .\run.ps1
If 503 persists, check the gateway terminal for provider backoff messages.
Cursor (page / hotkey errors, recovery loop)
Root cause: the electron layer called cua-driver page without window_id, and recovery replans injected hotkey steps without a pid.
Fixes applied:
â€¢ Electron layer resolves (pid, window_id) and passes both to all page / hotkey / type_text calls
â€¢ Electron action errors are caught instead of crashing the cascade
â€¢ Hotkey layer fails cleanly when pid is missing (no more uncaught CuaDriverError)
â€¢ Failed nodes report path=electron instead of path=read
Retry Cursor preset
1. Restart the UI (to load the code changes):
cd code
.\run_ui.ps1
2. Launch Cursor with CDP (separate terminal, once per session):
cd code
.\computer\scripts\launch_cursor_debug.ps1
3. Run Cursor â€” create evidence file again.
You should see path=electron in progress. If it still fails, the detail column will show the specific cua-driver or gateway error instead of a misleading path=read.
